channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi meets Pakistan Army chief Munir
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi met Pakistan Army chief Munir, with unclear prospects for direct US-Iran talks. There is no clarity on whether direct talks between Washington and Tehran will take place during the visit.

item.title: US Millionaire Killed By Elephant Herd While Hunting Antelope In African Jungle
item.description: Ernie Dosio, 75, was killed by elephants during an antelope hunt in Gabon's rainforest. Ernie Dosio, 75, is killed by a herd of elephants during an antelope hunt in Gabon's rainforest.

item.title: Iran Foreign Minister Araghchi meets Pakistan Army chief Munir
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi met with Pakistan's Army chief Munir, coinciding with the resumption of commercial flights from Tehran's international airport after a two-month hiatus. Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U. S.

item.title: Vance on 'standby' as Witkoff, Kushner to reach Pakistan on April 25 for talks with Iran, White
item.description: Iran ruled out direct negotiations with US representatives, ahead of talks in Pakistan on April 25. Iran made it clear that there would be no direct negotiations with American government representatives.

item.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
item.description: Seven people died in a Russian attack on Ukraine. Dnipro was hardest hit. Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine.

item.title: US envoys head to Pakistan for Iran war talks
item.description: US envoys are heading to Pakistan for Iran war talks. The US is "looking for a face-saving way to escape the war quagmire it has become trapped in", says Iran's defence ministry.

item.title: Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for first time since conflict
item.description: Iran resumed commercial flights from Tehran's international airport after a two-month halt due to conflict. Iran has resumed commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U. S.

item.title: ‘They’re making an offer’ amid Islamabad diplomacy push
item.description: Islamabad is pushing diplomacy, with reports of a potential offer being made. ‘They’re making an offer’ amid Islamabad diplomacy push.

item.title: The Elite ‘Doctors’ Who Care for Mount Everest
item.description: "Icefall doctors" are working to clear glacial ice blocking the summit of Mount Everest. Glacial ice has sealed the gateway to Everest’s summit, keeping hundreds of climbers from reaching the top.

item.title: Bangladesh issues nationwide security alert over possible militant attacks
item.description: Bangladesh issued a nationwide security alert due to possible militant attacks. "The alert has been issued based on intelligence reports,” a police headquarters (PHQ) official said, requesting anonymity. The official described the alert as “urgent and confidential”.

item.title: The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-year-old French widow of a former G. I., was deported from the US under Trump's immigration crackdown. In her first interview since being deported, she recounted her experience in ICE detention.

item.title: Judge delays Trump's US$10 billion lawsuit against US tax agency
item.description: US District Court Judge Kathleen Williams delayed Trump's US$10 billion lawsuit against the US tax agency. While Trump states "that he is bringing this lawsuit in a personal capacity, he is the sitting president and his named adversaries are entities whose decisions are subject to his direction", wrote US District Court Judge Kathleen Williams.

item.title: Gujarati Woman Shot At US Grocery Store, Husband Arrested For Illegal Gun Use
item.description: Sharad Gajera was arrested for illegal gun use after his wife was shot at a US grocery store. No court date has been set yet for Sharad Gajera. Gujarati Woman Shot At US Grocery Store, Husband Arrested For Illegal Gun Use.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Iran says 'enemy seeking face-saving exit' from
item.description: Iran stated the enemy is seeking a face-saving exit from the US-Israel-Iran war. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Iran says 'enemy seeking face-saving exit' from war; peace talks remain in limbo The Times of India.

item.title: Tunisia suspends rights group amid widening repression
item.description: Tunisian authorities suspended a rights group for a month amid widening repression and criticism. Tunisian authorities have ordered one of Africa's oldest rights groups to cease its activities for a month.

item.title: US, Iran To Hold 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today. What's On Agenda?
item.description: Iran and the US will hold a second round of peace talks in Pakistan. The previous round occurred on April 11-12, amid a delicate ceasefire. The previous round of talks was held on April 11-12.

item.title: Man Walks Calmly Among Mexican Ruins, Then Opens Fire Killing Tourist
item.description: Julio Cesar Jasso Ramirez opened fire at Mexican ruins, killing a tourist, and has been identified as the attacker. The attacker has been identified as 27-year-old Mexican national Julio Cesar Jasso Ramirez.

item.title: Sam Altman apologises after warning signs missed in Canada shooting
item.description: Sam Altman, CEO of Y Combinator, apologized for missing warning signs in the Canada shooting, citing a failure in the company's due diligence process. The incident has raised questions about the responsibility of tech companies in identifying potential threats.

item.title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
item.description: Iran's economic crisis has forced many to cross into Turkey to buy basic goods like cooking oil. The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.

item.title: Australia and New Zealand gather in Turkiye to commemorate WWI battle
item.description: Australian and New Zealand officials gathered in Turkiye to commemorate the WWI battle of Gallipoli. The event included mournful hymns, prayers, and the laying of wreaths by representatives from many countries.
